summary: Patrick reports his May 2007 financials for Bingo Card Creator, showing $600 in gross sales and $452 in profit after expenses. Sales were flat due to the school year ending, but he teases that June is already performing better than expected. He highlights that switching to e-junkie's cart system significantly boosted CD sales. The post is a brief monthly accounting update with a forward-looking tease about summer traffic trends.
key_points: Monthly revenue hit $600 with 22 sales including 10 CD orders, showing the physical product channel was meaningful
The e-junkie cart change dramatically improved CD sales conversion, demonstrating how small checkout flow changes impact revenue
Total expenses of $138 yielded healthy ~75% profit margins on a micro-scale software business
June was already tracking ahead of May despite expectations of a seasonal slowdown
money_quotes: note that that e-junkie cart change made ALL the difference!
I was expecting sales for June to be barely enough to cover my AdWords campaign. They are already over half of May's.
key_insight: Small changes to the purchase flow, like switching cart systems, can have outsized effects on conversion and revenue even for a tiny software business.
